practicals.title: Crystallization (Alum, Copper Sulphate, or Benzoic Acid)
practicals.unit_name: Characterization and Purification of Chemical Substances
practicals.details: Determination from impure sample.

practicals.title: Determination of pH using fruit juices/acid/base/salt solutions
practicals.details: Using pH paper or universal indicator.

practicals.title: Comparing the pH of strong and weak acids
Study pH change in titration using universal indicator
Study the pH change by common-ion effect
practicals.details: In case of weak acids and weak bases.

practicals.title: Volumetric Analysis (Titration)
practicals.details: Determination of strength using standard solutions.

practicals.title: Salt Analysis (Anion and Cation determination)
practicals.details: Detection in a given salt. Insoluble salts excluded.

practicals.title: Preparation of lyophilic sol (Starch, Egg Albumin, Gum) and Lyophobic sol
practicals.details: Class XII Practical Syllabus.

practicals.title: Dialysis of sol prepared in Surface Chemistry experiment
Study the role of emulsifying agents in stabilizing emulsion
Effect of concentration and temperature on reaction rate (Sodium Thiosulphate + HCl)
practicals.details: Class XII Practical Syllabus.

practicals.title: Study of reaction rates (Iodide ion with Hydrogen Peroxide or Clock Reaction)
Enthalpy of dissolution (Copper Sulphate/KNO3) or Neutralization
practicals.details: Class XII Practical Syllabus.

practicals.title: Variation of cell potential in Zn/Zn2+ || Cu2+/Cu with concentration change
Separation of pigments by paper chromatography and determination of Rf values
practicals.details: From extracts of leaves and flowers.

practicals.title: Preparation of double salt (Ferrous Ammonium Sulphate or Potash Alum)
Preparation of Organic Compound (Acetanilide, Di-benzal acetone, etc.)
practicals.details: Class XII Practical Syllabus.

practicals.title: Tests for functional groups (Unsaturation, Alcoholic, Phenolic, etc.)
Characteristic tests of carbohydrates, fats and proteins in foodstuffs

projects.title: Checking the bacterial contamination in drinking water by testing sulphide ion
projects.details: Scientific investigation involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.

projects.title: Study of the methods of purification of water
projects.details: Scientific investigation involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.

projects.title: Testing the hardness, presence of Iron, Fluoride, Chloride in drinking water
projects.details: Investigation depending upon regional variation to study causes of ions above permissible limit.

projects.title: Investigation of the foaming capacity of different washing soaps and effect of Sodium carbonate
projects.details: Scientific investigation involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.

projects.title: Study the acidity of different samples of tea leaves
projects.details: Scientific investigation involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.

projects.title: Determination of the rate of evaporation of different liquids
projects.details: Scientific investigation involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.

projects.title: Study the effect of acids and bases on the tensile strength of fibers
projects.details: Scientific investigation involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.

projects.title: Study of acidity of fruit and vegetable juices
projects.details: Scientific investigation involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.

formative_topics: s & p Block Elements: Trends and Properties (Unit 3)
The Gaseous State: Gas Laws and Deviations
General Principles and Processes of Isolation of Elements

structure_rules: No chapter wise weightage provided, but all chapters must be covered.
Suitable internal variations may be made for generating various templates.
There will be no overall choice in the question paper.
33% internal choices will be given in all sections.

domains.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts and answers. Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.
Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences... Present and defend opinions...

internal_assessment.policy: Schools can integrate formative topics with existing chapters as they align well.
internal_assessment.stress_reduction_note: Topics assessed only formatively to reduce academic stress while ensuring meaningful learning.

requirements: Record at least five of the listed experiments.
Format: Aim, apparatus required, simple theory, procedure, related skills, precautions.

project_assessment.nature: Scientific investigations involving laboratory testing and collecting information from other sources.
suggested_topics_class_xi: Checking bacterial contamination in drinking water (sulphide ion)
Methods of purification of water
Testing hardness/Iron/Fluoride/Chloride in regional drinking water
Foaming capacity of washing soaps and effect of Sodium carbonate
Acidity of tea leaves samples
Rate of evaporation of different liquids
Effect of acids/bases on tensile strength of fibers
Acidity of fruit/vegetable juices
suggested_topics_class_xii: Presence of oxalate ions in guava at ripening stages
Quantity of casein in milk samples
Preparation and comparison of soybean milk with natural milk
Effect of Potassium Bisulphate as food preservative
Digestion of starch by salivary amylase (pH/temperature effect)
Rate of fermentation: wheat flour, gram flour, potato juice, carrot juice
Extraction of essential oils from Saunf/Ajwain/Illaichi
Study of common food adulterants

structure_rules: No chapter wise weightage provided, but all chapters must be covered.
Suitable internal variations may be made for generating various templates.
There will be no overall choice in the question paper.
33% internal choices will be given in all sections.

domains.description: Exhibit memory of previously learned material by recalling facts, terms, basic concepts and answers. Demonstrate understanding of facts and ideas.
Solve problems to new situations by applying acquired knowledge, facts, techniques and rules in a different way.
Examine and break information into parts by identifying motives or causes. Make inferences... Present and defend opinions...

internal_assessment.policy: Schools can integrate formative topics with existing chapters as they align well.
internal_assessment.stress_reduction_note: Topics assessed only formatively to reduce academic stress while ensuring meaningful learning.

requirements: Record at least five of the listed experiments.
Format: Aim, apparatus required, simple theory, procedure, related skills, precautions.

Study of common food adulterants

long_chapter_names_reclassified: Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques

competency_groups.title: Fundamental Chemical Concepts and Measurement
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to understanding the nature of matter, measurement principles, stoichiometry, and atomic theory.
competencies: Identify patterns in chemical phenomena at macroscopic, molecular, and symbolic levels.
Apply laws of chemical combination and Dalton's Atomic Theory to explain material properties.
Perform accurate measurements with awareness of uncertainty and significant figures.
Calculate percentage composition, mole concept applications, and stoichiometric quantities.

competency_groups.title: Atomic Structure and Quantum Mechanics
competency_groups.description: Competencies regarding the evolution of atomic models, sub-atomic particles, and quantum mechanical descriptions.
competencies: Trace the historical development from discovery of sub-atomic particles to Bohr's model.
Explain the transition towards the quantum mechanical model of the atom.
Apply quantum numbers to describe electron configurations in atoms.

competency_groups.title: Periodic Classification and Trends
competency_groups.description: Competencies focused on the logic behind element classification, periodic law, and trends in elemental properties.
competencies: Justify the need for classifying elements and explain the genesis of periodic classification.
Interpret the modern periodic table structure including s-, p-, d-, f- blocks.
Predict and analyze periodic trends in physical and chemical properties across periods and groups.
competency_groups.unit_name: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties
competency_groups.chapter_name: Classification of Elements and Periodicity in Properties

competency_groups.title: Chemical Bonding Theories
competency_groups.description: Competencies involving various theories explaining bond formation, molecular geometry, and bonding types.
competencies: Apply Kossel-Lewis approach to predict ionic and covalent bond formation.
Use VSEPR theory to determine molecular shapes based on electron pair repulsion.
Differentiate between Valence Bond Theory (hybridization) and Molecular Orbital Theory.

competency_groups.title: Thermodynamic Principles and Spontaneity
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to energy changes, enthalpy measurements, and criteria for reaction spontaneity.
competencies: Measure internal energy (∆U) and enthalpy change (∆H) using calorimetry techniques.
Classify reactions based on enthalpy changes (exothermic/endothermic).
Evaluate the spontaneity of processes using Gibbs Energy Change and equilibrium conditions.

competency_groups.title: Chemical Equilibrium Dynamics
competency_groups.description: Competencies concerning dynamic equilibrium, equilibrium constants, and factors affecting system stability.
competencies: Distinguish between physical and chemical equilibria as dynamic processes.
Apply the Law of Chemical Equilibrium to calculate Kc and Kp values.
Analyze the relationship between Gibbs energy (G), reaction quotient (Q), and equilibrium constant (K).
Predict shifts in equilibrium due to changes in concentration, temperature, or pressure.

competency_groups.title: Redox Reactions and Electrode Processes
competency_groups.description: Competencies involving electron transfer concepts, oxidation numbers, and electrochemical applications.
competencies: Identify oxidizing and reducing agents using classical definitions and electron transfer mechanisms.
Assign oxidation numbers to elements in compounds and ions to balance redox equations.
Relate redox reactions to electrode processes.

competency_groups.title: Organic Chemistry Fundamentals and Mechanisms
competency_groups.description: Competencies covering nomenclature, isomerism, structural representation, and reaction mechanisms.
competencies: Apply IUPAC nomenclature rules to name organic compounds systematically.
Represent structures using various formats (Lewis, condensed, bond-line) and identify functional groups.
Classify isomers and explain fundamental concepts of organic reaction mechanisms.
competency_groups.unit_name: Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques
competency_groups.chapter_name: Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles and Techniques

competency_groups.title: Hydrocarbon Classification and Reactivity
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to the properties, classification, and reactions of alkanes, alkenes, alkynes, and aromatics.
competencies: Classify hydrocarbons into saturated (alkanes), unsaturated (alkenes/alkynes), and aromatic types.
Analyze chemical reactivity patterns specific to different classes of hydrocarbons.

competency_groups.title: Solutions and Colligative Properties
competency_groups.description: Competencies regarding solution types, concentration expressions, vapor pressure laws, and colligative effects.
competencies: Express solution concentrations using various units (molarity, molality, mole fraction).
Apply Raoult's Law to ideal and non-ideal solutions.

competency_groups.title: Electrochemistry Applications
competency_groups.description: Competencies involving galvanic and electrolytic cells, Nernst equation, and corrosion.
competencies: Differentiate between Galvanic and Electrolytic cell operations.

competency_groups.title: Chemical Kinetics Analysis
competency_groups.description: Competencies focused on reaction rates, rate laws, and factors influencing speed of reactions.
competencies: Determine the order of reaction using integrated rate equations.

competency_groups.title: d and f Block Element Chemistry
competency_groups.description: Competencies covering transition elements, lanthanoids, actinoids, and their applications.
competencies: Correlate electronic configuration with general properties of d-block elements.
competency_groups.unit_name: Unit 4: d and f Block Elements

competency_groups.title: Coordination Chemistry Principles
competency_groups.description: Competencies regarding Werner's theory, nomenclature, isomerism, and bonding in coordination compounds.
competencies: Apply IUPAC rules for naming coordination compounds.

competency_groups.title: Organic Functional Group Chemistry (Haloalkanes to Amines)
competency_groups.description: Competencies covering nomenclature, preparation methods, and reactions of halogenated compounds, alcohols, carbonyls, and amines.
competencies: Predict chemical reactivity based on functional group nature.

competency_groups.title: Biomolecules and Biochemistry Basics
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to the structure, classification, and biological significance of carbohydrates, proteins, vitamins, and nucleic acids.
competencies: Classify biomolecules based on their structural characteristics.

competency_groups.title: Laboratory Techniques and Safety
competency_groups.description: Competencies involving practical skills in glass working, purification methods, pH determination, and safety protocols.
competencies: Execute basic laboratory techniques such as cutting, bending, and boring.

competency_groups.title: Analytical Chemistry Skills (Volumetric & Qualitative)
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to titration techniques, standardization, and identification of ions in salts.
competencies: Perform volumetric analysis including preparation of standard solutions.

competency_groups.title: Scientific Inquiry and Project Work
competency_groups.description: Competencies involving experimental design, data collection from multiple sources, and investigation of real-world chemical problems.
competencies: Design and execute scientific investigations to solve practical chemistry problems.
